mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for at least two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and capital expenditure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL momentum coincides with positive 5-day SPX returns in over 60% of observed cases during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and stronger corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction with a rank IC below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth dropping below 100,000 signals labor market deterioration and reduced wage growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth remains below the 100,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates inefficient economic expansion and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence metric fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When short-term rates exceed 3.5%, rising financing costs suppress industrial activity, and declining INDPRO confirms this drag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day SPX returns occur in over 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS is above 3.5% and INDPRO declines.
